name: Tibet (Snow Lion flag)

colors.symbolism: rays of the sun and the protectors of the Buddhist doctrine
alternating sun rays and the celestial blue
the sun, snow mountain and the lions; representing Tibetan Buddhism
the snow mountain (Kang-chen) and the lions

description: A triangular white snow mountain centred on a field of twelve alternating red and blue rays radiating from a yellow sun behind the mountain peak; in front of the mountain, two yellow snow lions face each other holding a flaming three-coloured jewel and a yin-yang gem; the entire design framed by a yellow border on three sides (top, hoist, bottom) leaving the fly open.
history: Designed under the 13th Dalai Lama and adopted in 1916. Used as the national flag of Tibet under the de facto independent government from 1916 until the Chinese annexation in 1959. Banned in the People's Republic of China but still used by the Central Tibetan Administration in exile and by Tibetan independence supporters worldwide.

features.symbolism: Snow mountain with two snow lions holding the three-coloured jewel and yin-yang gem, against twelve alternating red and blue sun rays, framed by a yellow bordure on three sides
